statement.p: You read on the internet that from the year 2010 to the year 2023, community colleges experienced over 37\% enrollment decline. In spring 2023 at Portland Community College, there were 25{,}957 students. Write an inequality where the solution set represents how many students may have been at PCC in spring 2010.

solution.p: We decide to let x represent the number of students enrolled at PCC in spring 2010. This is an initial value in the sense of (1.1.2). But we do not want to write an equation, because we were told that community colleges experienced over a 37\% enrollment decline, not exactly a 37\% decline. So we write the following:

But what inequality symbol makes sense here? If the decline was more than 37\%\text{,} then x started out larger than what it would have to be for x - 0.37x to equal 25957\text{.} So we need a greater than sign:
Once again, we will not try to solve this at this time.
